name: streams
context: Process event streams with Redis Streams: XADD, XREAD, consumer groups, acknowledgment
info: This skill covers Redis Streams: an append-only log data structure for durable event processing. Streams support adding entries with XADD, reading with XREAD (blocking or non-blocking), consumer groups for parallel processing with load balancing, acknowledgment with XACK for at-least-once delivery, and trimming with XTRIM or MAXLEN for bounded growth. Unlike [[redis/pub-sub]], streams persist messages, allow replay from any point, and guarantee ordered delivery. Consumer groups distribute work across multiple consumers, with pending entry tracking and automatic reassignment of failed consumers via XCLAIM.
triggers: add events to a redis stream
use redis as a message queue

sections.title: Adding and reading entries
sections.body: Use `XADD stream * field value ...` to append entries with auto-generated IDs. Read ranges with `XRANGE stream - +` (oldest to newest) or `XREVRANGE` (newest to oldest). Block for new entries with `XREAD BLOCK ms STREAMS stream $`. The `$` means "only new entries"; use `0` to read from the beginning. See [[redis#data-model]] for how streams fit into the Redis type system.

sections.title: Consumer groups
sections.body: Create with `XGROUP CREATE stream group startId`. Read with `XREADGROUP GROUP group consumer STREAMS stream >` where `>` means undelivered entries. Each entry goes to exactly one consumer. Use multiple consumers for parallel processing. The pending entries list (PEL) tracks unacknowledged entries. Monitor with `XPENDING` and reassign with `XCLAIM`. See [[redis/clustering]] for consumer group behavior across cluster nodes.

sections.title: Acknowledgment and recovery
sections.body: Acknowledge with `XACK stream group id` after successful processing. Unacknowledged entries remain in the PEL. Inspect pending entries with `XPENDING stream group`. Claim stuck entries from failed consumers with `XCLAIM stream group newConsumer minIdleTime id`. This provides at-least-once delivery semantics. Unlike [[redis/pub-sub]], no message is lost when a consumer is offline.

sections.title: Trimming and retention
sections.body: Use `XTRIM stream MAXLEN ~ count` for approximate trimming (efficient) or exact `MAXLEN count`. Trim by ID with `MINID ~ id` to remove entries older than a timestamp. Combine trimming with XADD using `XADD stream MAXLEN ~ count * field value` for automatic retention. Monitor stream length with `XLEN stream`.
